per_memory.cml_output:
F: Production Redis @ redis-prod-a.internal:6380; TLS enabled; maxmemory=4GB; eviction=allkeys-lru
F: Replica @ redis-prod-b.internal; async replication; lag typically <50ms
F: Backups → offsite object store nightly @ 03:15 UTC
D: Primary has RDB disabled & AOF off ∵ keep write latency <1ms @ p99
W: No disk persistence on primary (intentional)

F: Postgres cluster db-prod-01; v16.2; Ubuntu 24.04
F: data_dir=/var/lib/postgresql/16/main; WAL archiving=on; archive_command → /mnt/backup/pg-wal/ (NAS mount)
F: shared_buffers=8GB; effective_cache_size=24GB; connection limit=200
F: pgbouncer fronting port 6432; transaction pooling mode

D: sqlite-vec chosen over pgvector for Mnemos memory store ∵ embedded, single-writer, zero-deployment vector search for default config; decided 2026-02-13 during v6 planning
D: Alternatives rejected (pgvector, Chroma, Weaviate) ∵ all require separate server process
F: Qdrant available as optional backend for multi-node / higher QPS needs

D: Switched embedding model Ollama Qwen3 → FastEmbed multilingual-e5-large @ 2026-02-15 (v7.0)
∵ Qwen3 slightly higher raw cosine on English but 4x slower on CPU; poor Swedish handling; Swedish needed for production memory store
F: e5-large: 1024-dim; ONNX format; CPU-native; ~120 passages/sec on Ryzen 7 8-core desktop

C: Alex Johnson — primary ops contact @ data center provider; alex.johnson@dc-provider.example; available weekdays 09:00–17:00 CET
F: Escalation via ticket system; priority 2; tag 'ded-rack'
F: Our rack = A-12
W: Avoid main support line for rack A-12 issues ∵ they redirect anyway

P: Direct, playful tone in casual replies
P: Never use em dashes in writing projects → use hyphens or parentheses instead
P: Suggested text blocks wrapped in copyable code fences
P: Direct, proactive style over overly cautious hedging
P: Reviews = honest opinions, not validation

P: Email replies → concise, 3-5 sentences; casual tone; never include AI-generated footers
P: Internal Slack-style messages → 1-2 sentences usually sufficient
P: Long-form writing ✓ for documentation & RFCs; ✗ for routine correspondence

F: Ethereum wallet for project txns: 0x0000111122223333444455556666777788889999 (illustrative, not real)
F: Cold storage on hardware wallet; firmware v2.1.0; seed phrase in steel backup capsule stored offsite
F: Separate hot wallet exists for personal trading on different hardware device
W: Never share project wallet address outside project context

F: example-shop.com prod DB → host db-prod-01; MariaDB socket /var/run/mysqld/mysqld.sock; db name prod_shop; user shop_prod_rhclh7
F: staging DB → test_shop on same server (db-prod-01)
F: binlog format ROW; retention 7 days; size cap 50GB before oldest rotate

W: NEVER run db:migrate:fresh against production; production lacks safety trigger
F: staging box test_shop has trigger `prevent_fresh` → raises exception; production does not have this trigger
W: prior incident: lost 12 hours of customer messages ∵ db:migrate:fresh ran with wrong --env flag
W: always double-check APP_ENV before any migrate command

F: Jina rerank-v2 cross-encoder trained on query-document pairs; returns raw logits, not calibrated probabilities
F: Apply sigmoid to raw logits for usable threshold score
F: sigmoid(raw) >= 0.55 empirically correlates with "actually a duplicate"; below = noise
F: Threshold set as DEDUP_RERANK_THRESHOLD in mnemos.constants

L: Topic-level merge clusters (cosine 0.75–0.88) need more aggressive guardrails than tight near-duplicate clusters (>0.88) ∵ LLM over-generalizes & drops specifics when inputs loosely related
L: Hierarchical binary merge (introduced v10) mitigates this; merges only 2 memories at a time → "output ≈ one input size" intuition holds

F: Qdrant API key rotation steps: 1) generate new key: `openssl rand -hex 32`; 2) write to `/etc/qdrant/config.yaml` under `service.api_key`; 3) `systemctl restart qdrant`; 4) update `MNEMOS_QDRANT_API_KEY` in `/etc/mnemos/env`; 5) `systemctl restart mnemos-mcp`; 6) verify: `curl -H 'api-key: NEW' http://localhost:6333/collections`
W: Old key remains valid until rotation completes

F: Mnemos LongMemEval benchmark; dataset=longmemeval_s.json (cleaned, from HuggingFace); 470 non-abstention questions; 500-question full set
F: R@5 hybrid+rerank: 98.94% without CML; 99.15% with CML (against 470 non-abstention Qs)
F: End-to-end QA accuracy: 77.4% on full 500-question set; answerer=Sonnet; judge=Opus
F: All numbers reproducible from scripts under benchmarks/

F: FastEmbed e5-large cold start ~9s; RAM usage ~2.1GB
F: Inference throughput 120 passages/sec on Ryzen 7 8-core desktop w/ ONNX CPU runtime
F: Warm-up occurs at MCP server start; first memory_search after boot ~15ms ∵ model already in memory

F: Stripe webhook integration requires raw request body (byte-for-byte, as received) for signature verification
L: Signature verification failures caused by FastAPI middleware reading body stream before webhook handler → stream empty/parsed when Stripe verification attempted hashing
F: Fix: disable body parsing on /webhooks/stripe route specifically via dependency override
W: Easy to forget this requirement; initial debugging misled toward wrong secret or stale webhook URL; took ~3-4 failed attempts + ~1hr additional digging to find root cause

D: Stick w/ sqlite-vec as default vector store for Mnemos; ship pgvector as optional backend for later need
∵ sqlite-vec: rock solid, zero-deployment (lives in same SQLite file as memory data), no multi-node vector search needed at current scale; zero-deployment is a feature for single-user memory system
∵ pgvector argument: already running Postgres for app data, maintaining separate embedded vector index felt redundant
F: Postgres used for application data; sqlite-vec used for vector store + memory data in single SQLite file
L: Discussion took longer than warranted; occurred yesterday afternoon

F: Embedding pipeline benchmarks measured on ref box: Ryzen 7 8-core desktop; 64GB ECC RAM
F: FastEmbed loading multilingual-e5-large cold start ~9s; only once per MCP server boot
F: Model resident RAM ~2.1GB once loaded
F: Inference throughput: batch of 100 short passages → ~120 passages/sec; CPU-native ONNX runtime
F: First memory_search after startup ~15ms ∵ model already warm via MCP server warmup hook
D: Quote these inference numbers carefully/consistently going forward ∵ previously cited inconsistent figures

W: Jan 22 — ran `db:migrate:fresh` against example-shop.com production ∵ APP_ENV flag pointed at production instead of staging; lost ~12hrs customer messages
F: staging has `prevent_fresh` trigger (raises exception on destructive migration); production lacks this trigger → production executed without safeguard
D: Hard rule: NEVER run `db:migrate:fresh` without first reading APP_ENV & verifying target hostname; applies even when tired/rushed ∵ this incident

P: Casual tone preferred most of the time → playful direct style; avoid hedged corporate-speak ∵ "reads more like an actual person talking"
P: No em dashes in any writing project ∵ finds them lazy → use hyphens, parentheses, or end sentence instead
P: Suggested text blocks always in copyable code fences ∵ easy to lift into destination
P: Email replies → concise, target 3-5 sentences; never more unless specific reason
P: Reviews of user's writing → actual honest opinions, not validation; if answer is "it's great" user assumes not genuine feedback
